name: PyUSB (USB Device Access from Python)

auth.notes: Local library; requires libusb (libusb1 or libusb0) or OpenUSB backend

performance.notes: USB transfer speed depends on device type (HID, bulk, isochronous); not Python-limited

agent_readiness.notes: Access USB devices from Python via libusb. import usb.core. dev = usb.core.find(idVendor=0x1234, idProduct=0x5678). dev.set_configuration(). dev.write(endpoint, data). data = dev.read(endpoint, size). usb.core.find(find_all=True) → list all devices. dev.ctrl_transfer(bmRequestType, bRequest, wValue, wIndex, data). usb.util.get_string(dev, dev.iManufacturer). Detach kernel driver: dev.detach_kernel_interface(0). Endpoints: dev[0][(0,0)][0]. Bulk, interrupt, isochronous, control transfers. udev rules required on Linux for non-root access.

gotchas: Requires libusb installed (not in Python — system library)
Linux: non-root access requires udev rules (/etc/udev/rules.d/)
Windows: requires Zadig to replace WinUSB driver for device
macOS: SIP may block access to certain USB devices
No type annotations — poor IDE support
Endpoint addressing is verbose — must traverse configuration/interface/altsetting
best_when: Low-level USB device access for custom hardware, lab instruments, or HID devices
When no vendor SDK is available and raw USB protocol access is needed
Embedded systems communication (Arduino, STM32 via custom USB descriptors)
avoid_when: HID devices with standard usage pages (use hid or hidapi)
When vendor provides a Python SDK
summary: PyUSB provides raw USB device access from Python via libusb. usb.core.find(idVendor, idProduct). dev.write(endpoint, data). dev.read(endpoint, size). ctrl_transfer() for control transfers. Bulk, interrupt, isochronous support. BSD-3. Best for custom hardware and lab instruments without vendor SDK. Requires libusb system library. udev rules on Linux for non-root. Windows needs Zadig driver replacement. No type annotations.
